title: Use item accessors and correct ItemVars union arms
applies_when: The target function operates on an item GObj or Item data.
Generated output accesses gobj->user_data, ip->xDD4_itemVar, or article special attributes.
summary: Item code commonly wants Item_GObj*, GET_ITEM(gobj), the correct ItemVars union arm, and explicit special-attribute typing when evidence supports the type.
do: Use Item_GObj* for item function signatures when the caller/header evidence supports it.
Use Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj) when accessing item data from an item GObj.
Select the correct ip->xDD4_itemVar.<item> union member instead of accepting an m2c default.
Cast or type xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to the evidenced item attribute struct.
Update item header prototypes when the function body proves the signature.
do_not: Leave raw gobj->user_data temporaries as finished source when GET_ITEM represents the operation.
Use an unrelated ItemVars union arm just because generated output guessed it.
Define new attribute fields without offset, sibling-source, header, data, or objdiff evidence.
Treat this fact as stronger than current source, headers, assembly, or objdiff.
